element_introduction_order.first_appears: Prison ship Vortex Rikers escape sequence
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player finds weapon to escape initial Skaarj attack on ship

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Prison ship, immediately after weapon acquisition
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: First hostile encounter; Skaarj board ship and kill survivors

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Canyon and mine areas after ship crash
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Encountered as slaves; provide contextual storytelling through world state
Environmental storytelling element explaining Skaarj invasion motivation
Genetically engineered hostile forces; escalation of enemy types
Enemy faction serving Skaarj; adds tactical variety
Thematic environmental puzzle and combat arena
Major boss encounter requiring learned combat skills
Environmental storytelling; document other survivors' fates
Cultural and architectural progression showing Nali civilization
Skaarj technological structures with increased enemy density
Thematic callback to player's starting location; evidence of human presence
Gateway to final confrontation; contains teleporter to Skaarj mothership
Complex navigational structure with reactor destruction objective

typical_relief_triggers: Discovery of human journals providing narrative context
Safe passages through temples between combat zones
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating progression from escape scenario through exploration of alien-occupied world toward final confrontation with Skaarj leadership. Tension builds through enemy type introduction (Skaarj → Brutes → Krall) and location significance (mines → temples → mothership). Relief provided by peaceful Nali encounters and narrative discovery elements.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Human journals and electronic logs scattered throughout environments; Nali slave encounters signal safe areas

difficulty_curve.early_game: Low-moderate: Prison ship escape establishes basic combat; canyon and mine areas introduce enemy types (Skaarj, Brutes) in staged encounters with narrative breathing room via Nali slaves
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate-high: Water temple and Titan arena boss represent significant skill checks; enemy variety increases (Krall introduced); temple ruins create exploration complexity
difficulty_curve.late_game: High: Alien installations feature dense enemy concentrations; Skaarj Mothership labyrinth demands navigation mastery; final Queen encounter serves as climactic skill test
notable_spikes: Prison ship Skaarj ambush (early forced combat)
Titan arena boss fight (first major skill gate)
Mothership reactor destruction under darkness (navigation challenge)

notable_level_patterns.description: Campaign opens with forced combat escape (prison ship), transitions to exploratory traversal through hostile world. Establishes player agency and freedom after linear opening.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Vortex Rikers to canyon crash site

notable_level_patterns.description: Mines and processing facilities (functional spaces) lead to temples, arenas, and castles (designed combat spaces). Natural-to-constructed progression.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Tarydium mines → Water temple → Titan arena

notable_level_patterns.description: Destroyed villages, temples, and crashed ships serve as narrative anchors. Human journals detail fates; Nali architecture demonstrates civilization before Skaarj occupation.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Nali temple complexes and ruins scattered mid-campaign

notable_level_patterns.description: Major antagonists (Titan, Skaarj Queen) gate access to subsequent campaign phases. Spatial advancement tied to combat mastery.
Skaarj Mothership functions as complex navigational puzzle with reactor objective. Darkness mechanic introduces environmental challenge beyond enemy combat.
